source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design the primary pressure-shell module of a 4-crew Lunar Base Camp for a team space-design benchmark as a multi-part welded aluminum pressure vessel with regolith radiation cladding and internal stiffener frames. The assembly consists of the cylindrical barrel (longitudinally welded Al-2219 plate), a forward hemispherical endcap, an aft hemispherical endcap, barrel-to-endcap weld rings, internal stiffener ring frames, and the top-surface regolith-sandbag radiation cladding layer at 15 g/cm^2 areal density over the crew bunk area. Single cylindrical module with hemispherical endcaps; internal volume 50 m^3, outer diameter 4.0 m, cylindrical length 4.0 m, total length 8.0 m. Launch-mass cap per RFP baseline: 12,000 kg wet including shell + ECLSS + structure. Radiation areal density target (GCR+SPE): 15 g/cm^2 equivalent polyethylene or regolith sandbag layer atop Al shell. Shell material: Aluminum 2219-T87 (Young's modulus 73 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 352 MPa, ultimate 455 MPa, density 2840 kg/m^3), nominal wall 5 mm. Internal pressure MEOP = 70 kPa (hypobaric baseline), lunar external 0 kPa. Design factors per ECSS-E-ST-32-02C Rev.2 + ANSI/AIAA S-080A metallic crewed: yield FoS 1.25, ultimate FoS 1.4, proof = 1.5 x MEOP, burst = 2.0 x MEOP. Thermal cycle: -180 C (2-week lunar night) to +120 C (lunar day sun). Load cases: (LC1) MEOP 70 kPa internal; (LC2) proof 105 kPa; (LC3) burst 140 kPa must not produce ultimate failure; (LC4) launch quasi-static 9 g axial + 2 g lateral per SLS Block 1B; (LC5) thermal: external surface -180 C to +120 C, interior held at 22 C, with anisotropic radiation cladding on top surface; (LC6) lunar seismic 0.1 g horizontal. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1, max von Mises <= 281 MPa (yield 352 / 1.25), (R2) under LC1, max stress <= 325 MPa (ultimate 455 / 1.4), (R3) under LC3 burst no ultimate failure; (R4) under LC4 launch loads, max stress <= 325 MPa; (R5) radiation areal density over crew bunk area >= 15 g/cm^2 (computed from regolith sandbag + shell); (R6) shell + structure mass + cladding <= 8000 kg (reserving 4000 kg for ECLSS); (R7) first cylindrical mode >= 20 Hz. Verification is performed on the assembled pressure vessel with stiffener frames + radiation cladding tied to shell; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP AP242 with all bodies, FEA per pressure case on assembly, launch FEA, thermal gradient FEA, radiation shielding verification table, modal first 5 modes, and mass budget.
short_prompt:
Design the primary pressure-shell module of a 4-crew Lunar Base Camp as a multi-part welded aluminum pressure vessel with regolith radiation cladding and internal stiffener frames.

Single cylindrical module with hemispherical endcaps: internal volume 50 m^3, OD 4.0 m, cylindrical length 4.0 m, total length 8.0 m. Shell material Aluminum 2219-T87 (E 73 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 352 MPa, ultimate 455 MPa, density 2840 kg/m^3), nominal wall 5 mm. Top-surface regolith cladding at 15 g/cm^2 areal density over the crew bunk area. MEOP 70 kPa (hypobaric baseline); design to ECSS-E-ST-32-02C Rev.2 + ANSI/AIAA S-080A with yield FoS 1.25, ultimate FoS 1.4, proof 1.5x MEOP, burst 2.0x MEOP. Launch-mass cap 12,000 kg wet.

Load cases.
- LC1 MEOP 70 kPa internal.
- LC2 proof 105 kPa.
- LC3 burst 140 kPa (no ultimate failure).
- LC4 launch quasi-static 9 g axial + 2 g lateral per SLS Block 1B.
- LC5 thermal: external -180 C to +120 C, interior held at 22 C, with anisotropic top cladding.
- LC6 lunar seismic 0.1 g horizontal.

Shell + structure + cladding <= 8000 kg; first cylindrical mode >= 20 Hz.

prompt.title: Lunar Base Camp pressure module (multi-part welded assembly)
prompt.brief: 4-crew Al-2219 cylindrical pressure module split into barrel + two endcaps + weld rings + internal stiffener frames + regolith radiation cladding.

parts.name: forward_hemispherical_endcap
parts.role: forward hemispherical endcap welded to the barrel

parts.name: aft_hemispherical_endcap
parts.role: aft hemispherical endcap welded to the barrel

parts.name: barrel_to_endcap_weld_ring
parts.role: heavier-section weld ring at each circumferential weld joint

parts.name: internal_stiffener_frame
parts.role: internal ring frame resisting ovalization and launch lateral load

parts.name: regolith_sandbag_cladding
parts.role: top-surface sandbag stack providing 15 g/cm^2 areal density over crew bunk

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 4.0 m OD x 8.0 m total length
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: +Z along cylinder axis, +Y towards lunar zenith (cladding surface)

boundary_conditions.interfaces: barrel and endcaps joined at two circumferential weld rings with HAZ property reduction; internal stiffener frames welded / bolted to barrel inner surface; regolith cladding tied to external top surface as distributed mass with elastic contact
boundary_conditions.support: launch LC4 cradled along aft barrel and aft endcap; operational LC1-LC3 and LC5-LC6 on lunar cradle supports

prompt.objective: minimize shell + cladding mass under 8000 kg while passing yield, ultimate, burst, launch, thermal, and modal gates

interfaces.load_path: weld ring reinforcement at barrel/endcap joint
internal stiffener frame weld / bolt into barrel inner surface
regolith cladding distributed mass transferred to outer shell

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Crewed lunar modules are barrel + endcaps welded at two circumferential joints, stiffened internally by ring frames, and clad externally by regolith sandbag shielding; pressure, launch, and thermal gates all cross weld interfaces that must be modelled explicitly.
notes.speculation_caveats: Stiffener frame count and cladding coverage layout are speculative; materials, pressures, loads, and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.
